title: Build a basic LED circuit
description: Learn electronics fundamentals by safely wiring an LED with a resistor on a breadboard.

dialogue.text: Hey there! I'm Orion. Ready to see how simple electronics can be? We'll safely wire an LED with a resistor on a breadboard.

Grab a breadboard, two jumper wires, a 5 mm LED, a 220 ohm resistor, and a 5V power supply (battery pack or USB adapter). Keep the supply unplugged while wiring. The resistor limits current so the LED doesn't burn out. Ready to assemble?

Place the LED on the breadboard with the long leg (anode) on the positive rail. Connect the 220 ohm resistor in series, then use jumper wires to link the resistor to +5V and the LED's short leg to ground. Double-check polarity and keep the power supply disconnected until everything is secure. Once it's all set, connect the 5V supply and the LED should glow.
Great job! You've built your first circuit and lit up an LED. Always disconnect power before adjusting any connections.
